title: Multimodal learning with next-token prediction for large multimodal models

data_scope.evaluation_benchmarks: MSCOCO-30K, GenEval, T2I-CompBench, DPG-Bench, VBench, UCF-101, CALVIN

core_conclusion: Emu3, trained solely with next-token prediction on a unified discrete token vocabulary, matches well-established task-specific models across image generation, video generation, and vision-language understanding, without diffusion or compositional architectures. Consistent power-law scaling laws (R2 >= 0.99, data exponent beta=0.55) hold across all three modalities, and the framework extends to robotic manipulation and interleaved vision-language generation, establishing next-token prediction as a general-purpose foundation for unified multimodal intelligence.
why_it_matters: Collapses fragmented multimodal AI paradigms into one training objective; empirically establishes cross-modal scaling laws enabling principled compute planning; removes the encoder prerequisite by matching CLIP-initialized systems without pretrained vision encoders; extends to embodied AI without task-specific components; fully open-source (code, tokenizer, weights).
why_nature: Paradigm-level unification claim backed by empirical scaling law analysis (R2 >= 0.99); societal impact spans image/video generation, clinical AI, robotics, and geospatial intelligence; open-science commitment with full public model release.
added_value_to_my_research: Fine-tune Emu3's VQ tokenizer on satellite + census + POI sequences for a geospatial foundation model supporting health access prediction
Interleaved image-text generation enables temporally coherent urban health narrative maps — explainable AI outputs for policymakers
VLA framework transferred to city-scale mobility and EV charging dispatch as sequential vision-language-action decisions
Scaling law methodology (power-law fitting across modalities) provides compute-optimal recipe for training domain-specific geospatial models
Multi-task next-token objective consolidates 2SFCA, explainable ML, and facility-placement tools into a single fine-tuned foundation model

figures.caption: Emu3 framework — multimodal data tokenized into discrete tokens and processed by a unified Transformer decoder with next-token prediction, including robotic manipulation extension
Qualitative visualizations across T2I, T2V, future prediction, vision-language understanding, interleaved image-text generation, and embodied manipulation
Token-centric multimodal infrastructure and architectural comparisons with diffusion models and encoder+LLM paradigms on image generation and understanding tasks
Scaling laws of Emu3 — validation loss surfaces and predicted vs. observed curves for T2I, I2T, and T2V tasks confirming power-law behaviour (R2 >= 0.99)
Tokenizer reconstruction comparison — unified video tokenizer matches image tokenizer quality at 4x fewer latent tokens; strong advantage when matched by token budget
